hq: 2679 N Geranium Drive, Saratoga Springs, UT 84045

system_description.overview: Xerpa is a real-time AI sales copilot (AI CRO) that supports revenue teams before, during, and after every customer interaction. It provides live in-call guidance, automated follow-up, post-call summaries, and CRM sync to improve rep productivity and ensure consistent sales execution.

products.Real-Time AI Sales Copilot: Provides live, in-call AI guidance during sales meetings; detects objections, buying signals, and recommends responses in real time
products.Pre-Call Intelligence Engine: Automatically analyzes CRM, email, meeting notes, and past interactions; generates recommended talking points and risk alerts
products.Post-Call Automation Suite: Creates structured call summaries, action items, MEDDICC insights; syncs notes into CRM
products.Sales Enablement & Playbook Enforcement: Reinforces internal methodology with real-time prompts; highlights missed opportunities
products.Integrations Layer: Connects to Salesforce, HubSpot, Zoom, Google Meet, Gmail, Outlook for bi-directional sync and workflow automation

infrastructure.physical_presence: No physical offices; no physical servers — all cloud
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (report states 'spinning up multiple servers across all availability zones in AWS')

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Hosted in AWS VPC with segmented VPCs and access control lists (ACLs). System components configured so company and customer access is appropriately segmented from other customer accounts.
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on the application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols, and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS deployed via AWS to identify and protect against threats in conjunction with security policy network settings.
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access via bastion host with VPN.
network_architecture.tls: TLS with certificates from established third-party certificate authority
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server; all systems restricted to internet through this proxy
network_architecture.topology: User requests arrive encrypted via TLS; traffic passes through AWS VPC with segmented subnets and ACLs; IDS/IPS monitors threats; remote admin access via bastion host/VPN; production servers managed and protected by AWS with multi-AZ failover

application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments (CC8.1.4: changes developed and tested in separate dev/test environment before production)

databases: Not specifically named — AWS-hosted databases mentioned generically
data_storage.backup_frequency: Defined in Backup Policy (frequency not explicitly stated)

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: AWS IAM / Active Directory (passwords controlled through AWS password policy; AD mentioned for server/database accounts)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for user accounts with administrative access to production platform; MFA required for source code tool access; 2FA required for employee email
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination
authentication_access_control.password_policy: AWS password policy — periodic forced changes, password expiry, complexity requirements; accounts disabled after limited failed logon attempts

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service used to encrypt data at rest
encryption.key_management: AWS KMS (cloud provider key management service)

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Not named (source code management tool mentioned generically)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured — every merge request to move change to production requires additional approval (CC8.1.2)
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool used for baseline configs, implementation, and rollback (not named)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automated patch management set up by CTO/Management; monthly patch management reviews; workstations receive automatic updates and scanned daily for patch compliance; critical/security OS patches applied as available

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Monitoring tool (not named) — continuously monitors system capacity and performance; generates threshold alerts
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Not named (logging enabled for admin activities, logon attempts, data deletions at application and infrastructure level)
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts to IT management for high and critical risk events

third_party_services.purpose: Cloud infrastructure / data center services (subservice org)
CRM integration for bi-directional data sync
CRM integration for bi-directional data sync
Conferencing integration for real-time call support
Conferencing integration for real-time call support

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and malware protection software installed on all production servers (auto-scan continuous basis; definitions updated weekly); also on Mac workstations/laptops
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications; scan frequency adjustable; management remediates high/critical findings
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — Physical access to data center facilities: AWS is responsible; out of scope
A1.2 — Environmental protections / backup processes: Xerpa has no office; AWS manages environmental controls
notable_controls: CC8.1.2: Branch protection enforced — every merge request to production requires additional approval
CC8.1.3: Source code changes logged, time-stamped, attributed to author; source code tool access restricted to authorized users with MFA
CC6.2.2: Quarterly user access reviews for all accounts including privileged users
CC6.1.4: Access revocation within one business day of termination
CC7.1.2: Automated monthly vulnerability scans on infra and applications
CC4.1.1: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
CC7.4.1: BCDR plan documented, reviewed annually, tested annually with scenario-based testing

infrastructure.rationale: AWS multi-AZ with VPC, IDS/IPS, and VPN. No CDN mentioned, no multi-region, specific regions undisclosed. No IaC tool named explicitly though configuration-as-code approach described.

application_architecture.rationale: No languages, frameworks, or compute services named. Separate environments confirmed, branch protection confirmed, but architecture pattern undisclosed. Very limited technical disclosure.

data_layer.rationale: No specific databases, caching, or storage services named. Multi-AZ confirmed, backup policy exists, data classification policy in place, per-tenant segregation confirmed. No retention period or RPO specified.

security_posture.rationale: Monthly vuln scans, annual external pen test, IDS/IPS, endpoint protection, MFA on admin and source code, quarterly access reviews, 1-day revocation SLA, antivirus on all systems. No WAF, no SIEM named.

devops_maturity.rationale: Branch protection, separate environments, emergency change process, automated patch management confirmed. But CI/CD tool, IaC tool, and source control platform not named — significant disclosure gap for a 2025 report.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy exists, reviewed and tested annually with scenario-based testing, multi-AZ failover capability. No RTO/RPO disclosed, no multi-region failover, backup frequency not stated.

vendor_diversity.rationale: AWS is single critical cloud dependency (carve-out). Multiple SaaS integrations (Salesforce, HubSpot, Zoom, Google Meet) are critical for product function. Manageable concentration but heavy AWS reliance.

overall.rationale: Solid foundational security posture with multi-AZ AWS, MFA enforcement, IDS/IPS, quarterly access reviews, annual pen test, and tested BCDR. Major gap is extremely limited technical disclosure — no named CI/CD tool, no specific databases, no IaC tool, no cloud regions, no languages/frameworks. Early-stage company with appropriate controls for its stage.

red_flags: No specific databases, storage services, or caching technologies disclosed — cannot assess data layer depth
No CI/CD tool, IaC tool, or source control platform named — unusually opaque for a modern cloud product
No RTO or RPO disclosed in BCDR section
Single cloud provider (AWS) with carve-out method — no multi-region failover capability
Backup frequency not specified anywhere in the report
yellow_flags: No WAF mentioned despite being a web-based application
No SIEM named — logging and alerting appear to be basic AWS-native tooling
Trust criteria scope is Security-only — Availability and Confidentiality not covered despite cloud SaaS product
No CDN mentioned despite serving real-time features to distributed sales teams
Processing Integrity and Privacy explicitly excluded by management as 'not relevant' — notable for a product that processes CRM data and call recordings
green_flags: Multi-AZ AWS deployment with documented failover capability across availability zones
Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
Monthly automated vulnerability scanning with remediation tracking
Quarterly user access reviews including privileged accounts
MFA enforced on production admin access and source code tool
Branch protection requiring additional approval for all production merges
BCDR plan tested annually with scenario-based testing
Endpoint protection including antivirus on all production servers and employee devices
Network diagram included showing production AWS architecture
key_observations: Xerpa is a very early-stage company (SOC 2 Type 1 only) with a surprisingly thin technical disclosure — the report reads as a governance/policy document rather than a technical architecture document, which limits due diligence value

The product handles CRM data, email content, and live call recordings from enterprise sales teams — the Privacy and Processing Integrity trust criteria exclusions are a deliberate management decision that warrants scrutiny given the sensitive nature of the data
The desktop application component (led by Founding Engineer Tom Hester) suggests a client-side software component in addition to the cloud backend — security of the desktop app is not well covered
Content filtering through a proxy server is mentioned for employee systems, an unusually explicit control for a company of this size — suggests deliberate attention to insider threat
Accorp Partners (PAC-FIRM-LIC-47383) is the auditor — a smaller regional firm, which is typical for early-stage SOC 2 Type 1 reports
